title: cleome monophylla var. cordata
intro: Cleome monophylla var. cordata, also known as heartleaf spider flower or narrow-leaved spider flower, is a variety of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Cleome monophylla var. cordata include reducing fever, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
